mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor demand that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 70% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and consumer credit outstanding indicate a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, driving negative equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and RSAFS does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 55%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a month-over-month increase in the CPI index forces market participants to price in prolonged restrictive monetary policy, which suppresses forward equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day SPX returns occur in more than 60% of cases following CPIAUCSL increases exceeding 0.3% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect sustained consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically leads positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month forward window as consumer spending drives earnings growth.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST changes and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 in a single month, it confirms robust economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher in months where PAYEMS exceeds 200,000 compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where employment growth remains positive while industrial production contracts signals a structural shift toward services and away from goods, reducing manufacturing earnings pressure and supporting equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-positive/INDPRO-negative divergence fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where GDP growth decelerates below 1.5% annualized, any subsequent quarterly GDP decline confirms a technical recession, triggering a sharp negative equity return over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances following quarterly GDPC1 declines below -0.5%.
